creator: antoine@FreeBSD.org
text:
147554    clement 2005-11-08 09:02:51 +0000 (Tue, 08 Nov 2005) # FETCH_DEPENDS  - A list of "path:dir[:target]" tuples of other ports this
147554    clement 2005-11-08 09:02:51 +0000 (Tue, 08 Nov 2005) #                  package depends in the "fetch" stage.  "path" is the
147554    clement 2005-11-08 09:02:51 +0000 (Tue, 08 Nov 2005) #                  name of a file if it starts with a slash (/), an
147554    clement 2005-11-08 09:02:51 +0000 (Tue, 08 Nov 2005) #                  executable otherwise.  make will test for the
147554    clement 2005-11-08 09:02:51 +0000 (Tue, 08 Nov 2005) #                  existence (if it is a full pathname) or search for
147554    clement 2005-11-08 09:02:51 +0000 (Tue, 08 Nov 2005) #                  it in your $PATH (if it is an executable) and go
147554    clement 2005-11-08 09:02:51 +0000 (Tue, 08 Nov 2005) #                  into "dir" to do a "make all install" if it's not
147554    clement 2005-11-08 09:02:51 +0000 (Tue, 08 Nov 2005) #                  found.  If the third field ("target") exists, it will
297274       miwi 2012-05-23 08:17:49 +0000 (Wed, 23 May 2012) #                  be used instead of ${DEPENDS_TARGET}.  The first field
297274       miwi 2012-05-23 08:17:49 +0000 (Wed, 23 May 2012) #                  also supports a package name with a version range, in
297274       miwi 2012-05-23 08:17:49 +0000 (Wed, 23 May 2012) #                  the form package>=1.2 if a particular version is desired.

According to svn blame,  the path and the executable syntax are like this since at least november 2005.
The package syntax is like this since at least may 2012.
